annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4212577521,"number":57812,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Playwright] Docs: Update README with key concepts, code snippets, and new 1.1.0-beta.1 API features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` **Service directory:** `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/` **Triggered by:** commit 56c4ac216f (PR #57806) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Version `1.1.0-beta.1` was released on 2026-04-06, upgrading the API version to `2026-02-01-preview` and adding: - `PlaywrightWorkspaceData.Identity` and `PlaywrightWorkspacePatch.Identity` properties of type `ManagedServiceIdentity` (managed identity support) - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties.Reporting` and `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties.Reporting` of type `PlaywrightEnablementStatus?` - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties.StorageUri` and `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties.StorageUri` of type `Uri` ## Gaps Found - The **Examples** section links only to a generic URL (`(aka.ms/redacted) with no package-specific code snippets demonstrating how to create/update/delete a `PlaywrightWorkspaceResource` - The **Key concepts** section is a single generic sentence with no description of the resource types (`PlaywrightWorkspaceResource`, `PlaywrightQuotaResource`, `PlaywrightWorkspaceQuotaResource`) - New 1.1.0-beta.1 API capabilities — Managed Identity configuration, Reporting enablement, and Storage URI — are not documented or demonstrated anywhere in the README - No `tests/Samples/` folder exists; there are no test-backed code snippets to populate the README Examples section - A non-standard "Documentation" section is present instead of following the canonical README template structure
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/README.md` (edit) Replace the current **Key concepts** section: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` library provides the following primary resource types: - **`PlaywrightWorkspaceResource`** — Represents a Microsoft Playwright Testing workspace. A workspace is the top-level resource that hosts your Playwright test infrastructure. You can create, update, and delete workspaces, as well as configure managed identity and enable reporting. - **`PlaywrightQuotaResource`** — Represents a subscription-level quota for Playwright Testing resources. - **`PlaywrightWorkspaceQuotaResource`** — Represents a workspace-level quota. Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` Remove the non-standard **Documentation** section (the block containing Quickstart, API References, and Authentication links under `## Documentation`) and instead incorporate those links into the **Next steps** section. Replace the **Examples** section: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Playwright workspace ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate ``` ```` ### Update a workspace to enable reporting and set storage URI ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage ``` ```` ### Configure managed identity on a workspace ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity ``` ``` For more samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ```` Replace the **Next steps** section: ```markdown ## Next steps For more information about Microsoft Azure SDK, see [this website]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Quickstart guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/mgmt_quickstart.md) - [API reference documentation]((docs.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Authentication with Azure.Identity](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) - [More Azure SDK for .NET samples]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/tests/Samples/Sample1_PlaywrightWorkspace.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright; using Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright.Samples { public class Sample1_PlaywrightWorkspace { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateOrUpdateWorkspace() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceCollection workspaces = resourceGroup.GetPlaywrightWorkspaces(); PlaywrightWorkspaceData workspaceData = new PlaywrightWorkspaceData(location); ArmOperation lro = await workspaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, workspaceName, workspaceData); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = lro.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created workspace: {workspace.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task UpdateReportingAndStorage() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = await resourceGroup .GetPlaywrightWorkspaces() .GetAsync(workspaceName); PlaywrightWorkspacePatch patch = new PlaywrightWorkspacePatch { Properties = new PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties { Reporting = PlaywrightEnablementStatus.Enabled, StorageUri = new Uri("https://.blob.core.windows.net") } }; ArmOperation lro = await workspace.UpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, patch); Console.WriteLine($"Updated workspace reporting: {lro.Value.Data.Properties.Reporting}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ConfigureIdentity() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = await resourceGroup .GetPlaywrightWorkspaces() .GetAsync(workspaceName); PlaywrightWorkspacePatch patch = new PlaywrightWorkspacePatch { Identity = new ManagedServiceIdentity(ManagedServiceIdentityType.SystemAssigned) }; ArmOperation lro = await workspace.UpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, patch); Console.WriteLine($"Identity type: {lro.Value.Data.Identity?.ManagedServiceIdentityType}"); #endregion } } } ``` For each snippet, the `` → README placeholder mapping is: | Snippet name | README section | |---|---| | `PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate` | Examples > "Create a Playwright workspace" | | `PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage` | Examples > "Update a workspace to enable reporting and set storage URI" | | `PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity` | Examples > "Configure managed identity on a workspace" | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/README.md` currently has these issues vs. the canonical structure: | Required section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Too generic — no resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ No code snippets, no package-specific content | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ⚠️ Too brief, missing useful links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Additionally, there is a non-standard `## Documentation` section that should be removed and its links folded into Next steps. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 playwright` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags and injects code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 playwright` — regenerates public API listing files (run if any public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ``` arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue documents gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` README and requests the addition of code examples and documentation for three new properties introduced in version `1.1.0-beta.1`: `Identity` (Managed Service Identity), `Reporting`, and `StorageUri`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` v1.1.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** `PlaywrightWorkspaceData`, `PlaywrightWorkspacePatch`, `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties`, `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties`
**Scenarios:**
- README `Key concepts` section is a stub with no service-specific resource type descriptions
- README `Examples` section contains only a generic link with no package-specific code snippets
- New 1.1.0-beta.1 features (`Identity`, `Reporting`, `StorageUri`) are not documented in the README
- No `tests/Samples/` directory or snippet-backed sample test files exist

**Root ask:** Expand the README with service-specific key concepts, add snippet-backed code examples covering workspace creation, managed identity configuration, and reporting enablement, and create sample test files under `tests/Samples/`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue (not a code bug), generated by an automated workflow after `1.1.0-beta.1` was released. No runtime reproduction is needed.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/README.md` to confirm gaps in `Key concepts` and `Examples` sections
2. Create `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/tests/Samples/Sample1_PlaywrightWorkspace.cs` with snippet-region test methods demonstrating workspace CRUD, managed identity, and reporting
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 playwright` to inject snippet code into README placeholders
4. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate the sample code compiles and tests pass

A very similar open issue exists: #57812 — covers the same package and documentation gaps, filed from an earlier run of the same workflow.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright`, which follows the Azure Resource Manager SDK naming convention
- **Service:** `Microsoft Playwright Testing` — Confirmed by the service directory `sdk/playwright/` and the label applied to the nearly-identical sibling issue #57812
- **Confidence:** High — Package name unambiguously identifies both the management category and the Playwright Testing service; label pattern confirmed by existing issue #57812
</details>
